informal_statement.text: Given a slice of closed-open intervals [start, end), return the maximum number of mutually non-overlapping intervals selectable from the input.
preconditions: for every input interval, start < end
required_properties: the returned count equals the size of some subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals from the input
no subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals from the input is strictly larger than the returned count
intervals sharing only an endpoint (interval i ends where interval j starts) are considered non-overlapping
edge_cases: all intervals mutually disjoint (result equals len)
all intervals overlap a common point (result is 1)
intervals touching at a single endpoint

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: every interval satisfies start < stop.
Feasibility: the returned count is witnessed by some subset of the input that is pairwise non-overlapping.
common_failure_modes: confusing index selections with value selections

semantic_units.description: Optimality: no pairwise non-overlapping subset of the input is strictly larger than the returned count.
common_failure_modes: stating only feasibility (admits the trivial return 0)

semantic_units.description: Endpoint convention: two intervals that share only an endpoint are non-overlapping.
common_failure_modes: using strict inequality instead of non-strict at the boundary

semantic_units.description: Empty input: the result is 0 when the input is empty.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ivs : Intervals), (∀ i ∈ ivs, i.start < i.stop) → ∃ (sel : List Nat), Feasible ivs sel ∧ sel.length = intervalScheduling ivs
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every input interval has strictly positive length, there exists a feasible selection whose size equals the returned count.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ivs : Intervals) (sel : List Nat), (∀ i ∈ ivs, i.start < i.stop) → Feasible ivs sel → sel.length ≤ intervalScheduling ivs
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every input interval has strictly positive length, no feasible selection is strictly larger than the returned count.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Interval), a.stop = b.start → NonOverlap a b
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Intervals touching at a single endpoint are non-overlapping.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: def Interval (α : Type) := { start : α // True }
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce an interval type (placeholder) to formalize intervals with a start point; end points will be added in subsequent obligations.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
structure ClosedOpenInterval (α : Type) where
  start : α
  end_ : α
  (h : start ≤ end_)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define closed-open intervals [start, end_) with the constraint start ≤ end.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def nonoverlap {α} [LinearOrder α] (I J : ClosedOpenInterval α) : Prop :=
  I.end_ ≤ J.start ∨ J.end_ ≤ I.start
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define when two intervals are mutually non-overlapping (disjoint) for closed-open intervals.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def pairwiseNonoverlap {α} [LinearOrder α] (s : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃I⦄, I ∈ s → ∀ ⦃J⦄, J ∈ s → I ≠ J → nonoverlap I J
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define pairwise non-overlap for a set/list of intervals.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def selectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (s : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Prop :=
  pairwiseNonoverlap s
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define what it means for a subcollection to be selectable: all chosen intervals are pairwise non-overlapping.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def maxSelectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Nat :=
  Nat.findGreatest (by
    -- placeholder: existence of a maximum over finite subsets
    exact ⟨0, by simp, by
      intro n hn
      exact ⟨n, by simp⟩⟩)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the maximum size of a selectable subcollection among all subcollections of the input (finite maximum).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def solve {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) : Nat :=
  maxSelectable xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Specify the intended algorithm result as the maximum number of mutually non-overlapping intervals selectable from the input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem solve_le_maxSelectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
  solve xs ≤ maxSelectable xs := by
  rfl
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value does not exceed the defined maximum selectable size.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem exists_selectable_sublist_of_size {α} [LinearOrder α]
    (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
    ∃ (ys : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)),
      ys ⊆ xs ∧ selectable ys ∧ ys.length = maxSelectable xs := by
  -- obligation: show the maximum is attained by some selectable subcollection
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a selectable subcollection (sublist) of the input whose size equals the maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem optimality {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
  ∀ (ys : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)),
    ys ⊆ xs → selectable ys → ys.length ≤ solve xs := by
  -- obligation: any selectable subcollection cannot be larger than the optimum
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Any selectable subcollection of the input has size at most the algorithm’s returned maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem selectable_sublist_inherits {α} [LinearOrder α]
    (xs ys : List (ClosedOpenInterval α)) :
    ys ⊆ xs → selectable xs → selectable ys := by
  -- obligation: pairwise non-overlap is preserved under taking subcollections
  intro hsub hsel
  unfold selectable pairwiseNonoverlap at *
  intro I hI J hJ hIJ
  apply hsel
  · exact hsub hI
  · exact hsub hJ
  · exact hIJ
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the whole input is selectable, then any subcollection is also selectable (non-overlap is inherited by subsets).
